message:
fix(codegen): Register pow as error-callable even in mixed tuple iterations

**Problem**: Float test had compile error - pow() returns !f64 but calls weren't
wrapped with try, causing "operator != not allowed for type '!f64'" errors.

**Root Cause**: When iterating over tuple with mixed callable types:
  for pow_op in pow, operator.pow:
The code detected operator.pow (function ref) and skipped ALL error-callable
registration to avoid .call() monomorphization issues. This meant pow_op calls
got no try wrapper, even though BOTH pow variants return !f64.

**Solution**: Always register pow as error-callable when detected, regardless of
mixed types. Both runtime.builtins.pow.call and operator.pow return !f64 for
ZeroDivisionError (0.0 ** negative), so both need try.

**Impact**:
- Float test: COMPILE → RUNTIME (progress - now compiles!)
- Still 3 runtime failures (float hangs, bool 3 fails, binop 9 Rat fails)

**Files Changed**:
- src/codegen/native/statements/control/loops/for_basic.zig: Remove !has_function_ref guard

**Next**: Fix Rat class dunder method calls (biggest remaining issue - 9 tests)
